State ol Caliiornia
George Deukmejian, Governor
'P il
j :; ^.. '$
MEMORANDUM
Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Jeannine English, Executive
Little Hoover Commission
May 23, 1990
FPPC Advice on Attorney Contract Renewal
Little Hoover Commission
1303 J Street, Suite 270 . Sacramento, CA 95814 . (916) 445-2125
lllr . .
Joannine L. English
E e.,.hte Arccbt
TO:
FROM:
DATE:
RE:
Please advise the Commission and myself on the impact of Government Code Sections 871oo-
87103 on the renewal of the Little Hoover Commission's attorney contract with our privately retained
general counsel. The contract will be considered at the June 21, 1990, business meeting of the
Commission.
By way of background, the Little Hoover Commission is a bipartisan entity in state government
mandated to investigate, hold public hearings, report on the economy and efficiency of executive
branch agencies, as well as recommend solutions to problems h identifies to the Governor and the
Legislature. lt is composed of four members of the Legislature and nine appointees (see generally,
Government Code Section 8501 et seo.).
I am employed by the Commission in an exempt position, which I have held since August, 1988.
For some years, the Commission has retained private legal counsel, Howard L. Dickstein, under
contract, to aid it in its investigations and internal operations, in lieu of the Attorney General, in
order to avoid the conflicts that arose when it investigated agencies represented by the Attorney
Commission on California State Government Organization & Economy


General. Mr. Dlckstein has been under annually renewed contracts slnce 1986, two years before
I became Executlve Direclor. The Commission ltsolf sel€cts the general counsel and approvos his
tixed price contracts on an annual basis, a process in which I do not participate. Since the date
of his last conl@ct renowal, ln July, 1989, Mr. Dlcksteln and I became married to each oth6r, a
fact which was disclosed to the Commission at the time.
Under these clrcumstances, please advise me what, if anything, both the Commission and I should
do consistent wlth our Political Relorm Act obligallons in respect io the contracl renewal process.
JLE/dh

sections.question: Please advise the Commission and myself on the impact of Government Code Sections 87100-87103 on the renewal of the Little Hoover Commission's attorney contract with our privately retained general counsel. The contract will be considered at the June 21, 1990, business meeting of the Commission.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the Political Reform Act implications under Government Code Sections 87100-87103 for renewing an attorney contract when the Executive Director has married the privately retained general counsel since the last contract renewal?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter TO the FPPC, not a response FROM the FPPC. No conclusion/advice section present. The document is formatted as a memorandum requesting guidance. The document lacks typical advice letter structure (Q/C sections). Confidence reduced due to document formatting and lack of clear response section.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the Little Hoover Commission's Executive Director seeking FPPC advice on whether there are Political Reform Act issues (sections 87100-87103) related to renewing the Commission's attorney contract, given that the Executive Director married the attorney since the last contract renewal in July 1989.
